stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/test_selection_test adds the audit repro: a same-file mutant that survives the narrowed `zig test src/range.zig` but is killed by the configured `zig build test` is now reported `killed` (not `survived`), with the configured re-verification command recorded as the killing command; a genuine survivor that survives both commands stays `survived` and records the configured command (passing) as evidence; and a needsConfiguredReverification unit covers narrowed vs configured-set selections. Before the fix the repro failed with `expected .killed, found .survived`. Full suite green (490 tests).

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: full self-mutation dogfood of zentinel's own runner/selection is gated to the Phase 5 stages (task 085); the soundness fix is deterministic and proven by behavioral tests that fail without it (a configured-suite-killed survivor is no longer reported survived) rather than by a fresh mutation run.

residual_risk: Re-verification covers the primary recorded mutant verdict and the derived survivor counts and mutation score. The additive safety-mode matrix (result.mode_matrix) still records per-mode statuses for the selected commands and is advisory, not the primary verdict; sound per-mode re-verification across the matrix remains a future hardening item.

Re-verification re-runs the full configured command set only for survivors of a narrowed selection, so a project whose narrowed selection produces many false survivors pays close to the unnarrowed cost for those mutants; this is the conservative, sound trade-off and never hides a survivor.

Soundness is enforced in the orchestration (run_command.zig) over the injected mutant runner; the production runner is wired by the presentation adapter, so the guarantee holds for any executor that classifies the configured commands faithfully.
